title: Measuring AI Impact
summary: Move beyond anecdotes by measuring time saved, quality changes, and adoption outcomes on real workflows.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson resets the measurement conversation away from vague impressions and toward observable workflow change.
lessons.title: Measure Work, Not Wonder
lessons.subtitle: The point of AI measurement is to understand workflow improvement, not to defend a trend.
lessons.objective: Define the kinds of impact that matter when evaluating AI-assisted workflows.
lessons.useWhen: The point of AI measurement is to understand workflow improvement, not to defend a trend.
lessons.whyItMatters: Teams often say AI is valuable without showing where the value appears. Measurement sharpens priorities and trust.

coreConcepts.title: Focus On Workflow Outcomes
coreConcepts.text: Time to final output, reduced rework, better coverage, and clearer communication are common outcomes.

coreConcepts.title: Keep It Close To The Work
coreConcepts.text: The most useful measures are attached to a specific workflow, not to AI use in general.

coreConcepts.title: Pair Numbers With Examples
coreConcepts.text: Quantitative signals are stronger when paired with before-and-after outputs.

workedExample.scenario: A team claims AI saves time but cannot explain where.
workedExample.approach: They measure one workflow: weekly status reporting.
workedExample.result: The claim becomes concrete and discussable.
workedExample.verification: They compare the measured workflow against the prior manual version rather than relying on memory.

doThisNow.task: Pick one AI workflow and define two measures that would actually matter for it.
steps: Choose one quality or reliability measure.

commonMistakes: Using only a story with no evidence.

roleVariants.manager: Model safe adoption through small pilots, clear examples, and lightweight team habits.
roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.
roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: With the measurement target defined, this lesson introduces lightweight ROI thinking that business ICs can use without building a finance model.
lessons.title: Use Simple ROI Thinking
lessons.subtitle: You often need only rough math to make a smarter decision.
lessons.objective: Estimate impact and cost without building an overly complex financial model.
lessons.useWhen: You often need only rough math to make a smarter decision.
lessons.whyItMatters: Simple ROI thinking helps prioritize workflows and communicate value credibly.

coreConcepts.title: Estimate Time Gain
coreConcepts.text: How much time or rework could the workflow save per cycle?

coreConcepts.title: Estimate Frequency
coreConcepts.text: How often does the workflow happen?

coreConcepts.title: Estimate Implementation Cost
coreConcepts.text: Training, setup, review, and change management all affect the true payoff.

workedExample.scenario: A team debates whether a new AI-supported recap workflow is worth standardizing.
workedExample.approach: They estimate minutes saved per meeting, meetings per month, and the setup/training effort.
workedExample.result: The value conversation becomes more grounded.
workedExample.verification: They sanity-check the assumptions after a short pilot rather than freezing the estimate forever.

doThisNow.task: Run a rough ROI estimate for one AI workflow you use or want to use.
steps: Estimate the setup and review cost.

commonMistakes: Ignoring quality risks that erase the saved time.

roleVariants.manager: Model safe adoption through small pilots, clear examples, and lightweight team habits.
roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.
roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: Before-and-after pilots come next because they create the simplest credible evidence when baseline data is weak or informal.
lessons.title: Use Before-And-After Pilots
lessons.subtitle: Pilots become persuasive when they compare the old workflow to the new one on the same task.
lessons.objective: Design simple before-and-after pilots for AI workflows.
lessons.useWhen: Pilots become persuasive when they compare the old workflow to the new one on the same task.
lessons.whyItMatters: Before-and-after evidence is easy for teams and leaders to understand, and it exposes tradeoffs better than abstract claims.

coreConcepts.title: Use Comparable Work
coreConcepts.text: Compare similar cases or repeated cycles of the same workflow.

coreConcepts.title: Capture Both Speed And Quality
coreConcepts.text: A faster workflow is not a win if the review burden or error rate spikes.

coreConcepts.title: Keep The Pilot Small
coreConcepts.text: A handful of cycles is often enough to learn whether the workflow has promise.

workedExample.scenario: A team tests AI-assisted customer recap emails.
workedExample.approach: They compare four manual recaps with four AI-assisted recaps using the same review standard.
workedExample.verification: They inspect both time saved and any mistakes caught in review.

doThisNow.task: Design a small before-and-after pilot for one workflow.
steps: Choose the measures you will capture.

commonMistakes: Running a pilot with no consistent review standard.

roleVariants.manager: Model safe adoption through small pilots, clear examples, and lightweight team habits.
roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.
roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: After evidence exists, this lesson focuses on communicating impact upward without overstating certainty or claiming unrealistic savings.
lessons.title: Communicate Impact Upward
lessons.subtitle: Leaders usually want evidence tied to outcomes, risk, and scale.
lessons.objective: Present AI impact in language that decision-makers can act on.
lessons.useWhen: Leaders usually want evidence tied to outcomes, risk, and scale.
lessons.whyItMatters: Good work gets ignored if the impact is communicated vaguely or without enough evidence.

coreConcepts.title: Lead With Workflow Outcome
coreConcepts.text: Start with what improved and for whom.

coreConcepts.title: Show The Evidence
coreConcepts.text: Use short metrics plus concrete before-and-after examples.

coreConcepts.title: State The Next Decision
coreConcepts.text: Should the workflow continue, expand, or stop? The communication should support that choice.

workedExample.scenario: A team has positive pilot results but struggles to explain them to leadership.
workedExample.approach: They present the workflow, the measured change, the risk controls, and the next step recommendation.
workedExample.result: The conversation becomes easier to advance.
workedExample.verification: They ensure every claim in the readout maps to collected pilot evidence.

doThisNow.task: Draft a one-page impact readout for one AI workflow.

commonMistakes: Leading with excitement instead of evidence.
Presenting impact without a next decision.

roleVariants.manager: Model safe adoption through small pilots, clear examples, and lightweight team habits.
roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.
roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: The module closes by turning the earlier pieces into a small recurring measurement system instead of a one-time case study.
lessons.title: Build A Small Measurement System
lessons.subtitle: Measurement works best when it is easy to maintain.
lessons.objective: Create a lightweight recurring measurement habit for AI workflows.
lessons.useWhen: Measurement works best when it is easy to maintain.
lessons.whyItMatters: If measurement is too heavy, it disappears. If it is too vague, it never helps decisions.

coreConcepts.title: Choose A Small Set Of Workflows
coreConcepts.text: Measure the workflows that matter most rather than everything.

coreConcepts.title: Use Simple Templates
coreConcepts.text: A recurring pilot sheet or evidence log keeps collection manageable.

coreConcepts.title: Review On A Rhythm
coreConcepts.text: A monthly or quarterly review is often enough to make better decisions.

workedExample.scenario: A team keeps losing impact evidence because every pilot is tracked differently.
workedExample.approach: They adopt one template and a monthly review habit.
workedExample.result: Comparisons become easier and evidence accumulates.
workedExample.verification: They check that the template still captures the most decision-useful data after a few cycles.

doThisNow.task: Write a one-page measurement rhythm for one AI-supported workflow you already use.
steps: Pick one output, one quality check, and one time or effort measure.
Choose how often you will review the workflow.
Write the rule for when you will keep, change, or stop the workflow.

commonMistakes: Collecting evidence but never reviewing it.

roleVariants.manager: Model safe adoption through small pilots, clear examples, and lightweight team habits.
roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.
roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

outcomes: Communicate AI value with stronger evidence.
nextModuleBridge: Next, end the core program by building a sustainable system for staying current without drowning in noise.

resources.title: NIST AI RMF Playbook
resources.note: Practical reference for governance, measurement, and risk-management language that stays more durable than vendor marketing.
